nodes.label: One Put per Key per Transaction
nodes.norm_label: one put per key per transaction

nodes.label: One Write per Key per Transaction
nodes.rationale: Rejecting same-key reuse makes timestamp and version addressing well-defined instead of silently losing an intermediate history row.
nodes.norm_label: one write per key per transaction

nodes.label: Checkpoint Manifest-Chunks Junction Table
nodes.rationale: A btree-indexed junction table replaces an array/GIN design that could not accelerate the scalar membership anti-join used by garbage collection.

nodes.label: Full VersionedEntry Property Comparison
nodes.rationale: Property P4 compares the complete versioned record so equal adjacent values cannot hide a broken version or timestamp mapping.

nodes.label: MongoDB Dependency and Nix Build Problem
nodes.rationale: MongoDB is compiled from source in the target Nix environment, adds a second service and database technology, and supplies capabilities this small custom store does not need.
nodes.norm_label: mongodb dependency and nix build problem

nodes.label: FerretDB Compatibility-Shim Rejection
nodes.rationale: FerretDB was rejected because its immature transaction and large-blob semantics are weakest in the exact multi-document checkpoint patterns UmbraDB needs.

nodes.label: Tier-1-Only Scope
nodes.rationale: The rebuild changes client persistence only; the separate Tier-2 indexer and TimescaleDB design remains unchanged.

nodes.label: External Claim Verification Rule
nodes.rationale: Every dependency or API claim must be checked against installed types or version-pinned upstream sources because prior reviews found plausible but incorrect assumptions.

nodes.label: OpenSpec review cadence (Opus panel + Fable 5 + Codex audit)
nodes.norm_label: openspec review cadence (opus panel + fable 5 + codex audit)
